# qwik-umami

> SSR-safe Umami Analytics component and hook for Qwik

## What this package does

qwik-umami provides two exports for integrating Umami analytics into a Qwik application:

1. `<UmamiScript>` — A Qwik component that loads the Umami tracker script client-side
2. `useUmamiTrack()` — A hook that returns a `track()` function for custom events

Page views are tracked automatically by Umami's script. Custom events use `track()`.

## Installation

```bash
bun add qwik-umami
npm install qwik-umami
pnpm add qwik-umami
```

Peer dependency: `@builder.io/qwik >= 1.0.0`

## Exports

```ts
import { UmamiScript, useUmamiTrack } from 'qwik-umami';
import type { UmamiConfig, UmamiEventData } from 'qwik-umami';
```

## UmamiScript component

Place in `<head>` inside `root.tsx`. This is the only setup needed for automatic page view tracking.

```tsx
import { UmamiScript } from 'qwik-umami';

// In root.tsx, inside <head>:
<UmamiScript websiteId="your-website-id" />

// Self-hosted:
<UmamiScript
  websiteId="your-website-id"
  src="https://your-umami.example.com/script.js"
/>
```

### Props (UmamiConfig)

| Prop | Type | Default | Required | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| websiteId | string | — | yes | Umami website ID (UUID) |
| src | string | https://cloud.umami.is/script.js | no | Tracker script URL |
| hostUrl | string | — | no | Custom data collection endpoint |
| autoTrack | boolean | true | no | Auto-track page views |
| domains | string | — | no | Comma-separated allowed domains |
| dataDomains | string | — | no | Comma-separated data domains |

### Implementation details

- Uses `useVisibleTask$` (client-side only, SSR-safe)
- Checks if script is already loaded via `data-website-id` attribute (dedup)
- Sends HEAD request to verify service availability before injecting script (3s timeout)
- Fails silently if service is unreachable or script is blocked

## useUmamiTrack hook

Returns `{ track }` function. SSR-safe (no-op on server).

```tsx
import { useUmamiTrack } from 'qwik-umami';

const { track } = useUmamiTrack();

// Simple event
track('button-click');

// Event with data
track('signup', { plan: 'pro', source: 'landing' });
```

### track() signature

```ts
track(eventName: string, eventData?: Record<string, string | number | boolean>): void
```

### Behavior

- If `typeof window === 'undefined'`: returns immediately (SSR)
- If `window.umami` is not available: logs warning in dev, silent in prod
- If error occurs: catches and logs in dev, silent in prod
- Always fire-and-forget, never throws

## UmamiEventData type

```ts
type UmamiEventData = Record<string, string | number | boolean>;
```

Umami constraints: numbers max 4 decimal places, strings max 500 chars, objects max 50 properties.

## Common usage patterns

### Root setup (required)
```tsx
// src/root.tsx
<head>
  <UmamiScript websiteId="abc-123" />
</head>
```

### CTA tracking
```tsx
onClick$={() => track('cta-click', { location: 'hero', label: 'signup' })}
```

### Form submission tracking
```tsx
track('contact-form-submit', { service: 'consulting' });
```

### Auth event tracking
```tsx
track('login', { success: true });
track('register', { success: false });
```

### Feature/action tracking
```tsx
track('project-create');
track('project-open', { status: 'ACTIVE' });
track('chat-message-sent', { hasAttachments: true });
```

## Source structure

```
src/
├── index.ts                    # Barrel export
├── types.ts                    # UmamiConfig, UmamiEventData, Window augmentation
├── components/
│   └── umami-script.tsx        # <UmamiScript> component
└── hooks/
    └── use-umami-track.ts      # useUmamiTrack() hook
```

## Important notes for LLMs

- This package has NO runtime dependencies. Only `@builder.io/qwik` as peer dep.
- The `track()` function from `useUmamiTrack()` is NOT a QRL ($). It's a plain function that can be called inside `$()`, `onClick$()`, or any other Qwik handler.
- `<UmamiScript>` renders `null` — it only has a side effect (injecting the script tag).
- The package works with both Umami Cloud and self-hosted Umami instances.
- Do NOT use `data-umami-event` HTML attributes with this package — use the `track()` function from `useUmamiTrack()` instead, as it integrates properly with Qwik's event system.
